project_title: Counter-Terror Analysis - 13-page counter-terror analytical narrative arc
product_name: Counter-Terror Analysis - 13-page counter-terror analytical narrative arc
final_object_type: analytic method or human-AI evaluation module
runtime_target: structured public page plus project-specific data packet and proof runner
operational_end_state: A user can apply a method, compare baseline and method-supported reasoning, record human judgment, and preserve the claim boundary.
minimum_operational_release: A usable instrument for Counter-Terror Analysis - 13-page counter-terror analytical narrative arc: sample input, analytic rule, analyst or model action, output, scoring or review gate, and falsification test.
full_build_definition: An instrument with input, rule or rubric, analyst action, scoring or classification output, replay log, falsifier, and evidence-bound acceptance criteria.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 5 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
public_claim_now: May claim a prototype-to-full-build path exists; do not claim completed operational system.
current_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
blocking_gates: live capture and outside-use evidence before deployment or validation claims
first_full_build: Add a compact source-to-judgment example: input source, analytic instrument, inference made, rival interpretation, confidence limit, and downstream use.
launch_or_review_path: Open the instrument contract for `row8`, run the sample input through the rule/rubric, and capture the output and falsifier.

instruments.purpose: Turn the `baseline answer` module into a concrete input-operation-output step for Counter-Terror Analysis - 13-page counter-terror analytical narrative arc.
instruments.input_contract: Project fixture, source duty, current claim boundary, and required output state.

instruments.output_contract: A reviewer-visible state record, output artifact, or decision boundary tied to the project acceptance tests.
instruments.acceptance_condition: The baseline answer instrument is present, named, connected to the fixture, and has an output or decision field that can be reviewed.

acceptance_tests: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence
source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation
privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
human-AI delta test: structured human review changes, narrows, blocks, or justifies output

export_schema.instrument_results: one result object per required module
